reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gates 1-6 all passed. Code verification confirms deduplication uses blockRootHex (hash of full BeaconBlockHeader including bodyRoot) instead of spec-required tuple (slot, proposer_index, index). An attacker controlling a validator can trigger this by creating two blocks at the same slot with different bodies (equivocation). Both data column sidecars for the same column index pass validation (valid signatures, KZG proofs) and are stored separately, violating the spec's anti-equivocation requirement from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json lines 280-296. However, the impact is limited to gossip-layer inefficiency (duplicate storage, network amplification) rather than consensus failure. Block-level equivocation handling exists separately. Severity remains Medium per original assessment - this is a spec compliance issue with bounded resource impact, not a critical consensus vulnerability.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Deduplication key (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) must prevent duplicate acceptance. [01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:281]
